activities_projects.title: Identifying the Invention
activities_projects.objective: Understand the main object and purpose of the invention in the story
steps: Read the first three paragraphs of the story.
Write down the name of the 'Great Invention' described by the brother.
List two things the invention is supposed to do (e.g., save time, help with chores).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify the central plot element and its functions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the invention and its uses (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Inventor's Sketchbook
activities_projects.objective: Visualize a description from the text
steps: Find the part of the story where the brother describes what the machine looks like.
Draw a picture of the invention based on those details.
Label three parts of your drawing using words from the story (e.g., 'wheels', 'lever', 'buttons').
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice descriptive reading and visualization.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and accuracy to the textual description (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Brotherly Love and Humour
activities_projects.objective: Identify the relationship between the characters
steps: How does the narrator (the sibling) feel about the brother's invention at first?
Write down one funny thing the brother says while explaining his machine.
Write two sentences on whether you think the invention will actually work.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize character emotions and the 'Wit and Humour' theme.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of character perspective and identification of humor (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sequence of the 'Great Plan'
activities_projects.objective: Understand the order of events in the story
steps: Put these events in the correct order: 1. The machine is tested. 2. The brother explains the idea. 3. Something goes wrong. 4. The narrator is curious.
Rewrite the sentences in a numbered list in your notebook.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to sequence narrative events chronologically.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct ordering of the story events (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Invention Words
activities_projects.objective: Learn words related to science and creativity
steps: Find the words 'Invention', 'Blueprint', 'Genius', and 'Disaster' in the text.
Write the meaning of each word in simple English.
Create a short sentence for each word: 'My brother thinks he is a ___.'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand their vocabulary within the context of the chapter.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and grammatical sentence structure (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Oops!' Moment
activities_projects.objective: Identify the climax or funny turning point of the story
steps: Identify the exact moment when the invention fails or behaves strangely.
Write 3 sentences describing what happened and why it was funny.
Share your description with a partner.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify humor through situational irony (failure of a 'great' plan).
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of description and focus on the funny element (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Synonym Matching
activities_projects.objective: Practice finding similar words in the text
steps: Find a word in the story that means the same as 'Funny' (e.g., 'Amusing').
Find a word that means the same as 'Smart' (e.g., 'Clever').
Find a word that means the same as 'Build' (e.g., 'Construct').
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve their understanding of synonyms.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of synonyms from the text (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role-Play: The Interview
activities_projects.objective: Practice speaking skills through character perspectives
steps: One student plays the 'Inventor' and another plays a 'Reporter'.
The reporter asks: 'What does your machine do?' and 'Is it safe?'
The inventor gives a funny answer based on the story.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop oral fluency and imaginative thinking.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and use of story details in dialogue (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Adjective Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Identify describing words in the poem or story
steps: Go through the description of the invention.
Circle 5 adjectives (e.g., 'shiny', 'clunky', 'noisy', 'great').
Write them in a list and choose one to describe your favorite toy.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the role of adjectives in descriptive writing.

activities_projects.title: My Own Great Invention
activities_projects.objective: Apply the theme of creativity to a personal idea
steps: Imagine you are the brother's assistant. What is one 'Great Invention' you would make?
Give it a funny name (e.g., 'The Homework-Eater 3000').
Write 2 sentences about what it does and draw a small icon for it.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect the story's theme to their own imagination.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and adherence to the 'wit and humour' theme (10 marks)
